resume_analysis.name: Priya Sharma
resume_analysis.summary: Backend engineer with 2 years experience building payment microservices and REST APIs in Python/Node.js with AWS deployments.

interview_sample.q1_warmup: Tell me about the payment microservice you built — what was the most technically challenging part?
interview_sample.q3_technical: How does Redis caching work and what cache invalidation strategy did you use in your project?
interview_sample.q6_tough: Design a rate-limiting system for an API that serves 1 million requests/day.
interview_sample.q9_edge_case: What happens to in-flight transactions if your payment service crashes mid-operation? How would you ensure consistency?

feedback_sample.summary: Strong practical backend experience with real production systems. Good understanding of caching and async patterns. System design knowledge needs deepening for senior roles.
feedback_sample.communication_insights: Spoke at a steady 130 wpm with good clarity. Occasional hesitation on unfamiliar terms — recommend practising think-aloud problem solving.
recommended_resources: System Design Interview by Alex Xu
